CREATE TABLE test_nested (id Int32, value Float64);
INSERT INTO test_nested VALUES (1, 1.0), (2, -INFINITY), (3, INFINITY), (4, NaN);
SELECT id, value, isInfinite(value) AS is_infinite FROM test_nested;
CREATE TABLE test_aggregates AS SELECT value, COUNT(*) AS count FROM test_float GROUP BY value;
SELECT DISTINCT isInfinite(value) FROM (SELECT value FROM test_float) AS subquery;

SELECT t1.value, t2.is_infinite FROM (SELECT value, isInfinite(value) AS is_infinite FROM test_float) AS t1 JOIN (SELECT id, isInfinite(value) AS is_infinite FROM test_nested) AS t2 ON t1.value = t2.id;
WITH (SELECT value FROM test_float) AS temp SELECT isInfinite(value) FROM temp WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT value, isInfinite(value) FROM (SELECT value FROM test_float WHERE value < 0) AS subquery;
